description:
What comes with $TRUST?

• Transparency
• Fairness

Are you tired of getting rugged? I am too. That’s exactly why I created $TRUST, a project built on full transparency, starting with the tokenomics.

The team holds 56% of the total supply. Out of this:

• 50% is fully staked on Printr
• 3.5% is allocated for marketing and future exchange listings
• 2.5% is reserved as team supply

You might be wondering: How is this fair if 50% of the supply is staked, meaning the team would earn most of the rewards?

It’s simple. The top 75 stakers will receive 2.5x their staking rewards.

For example:
If you earn 1 SOL in staking rewards over 12 hours, you will automatically receive 2.5 SOL from us.

Distributions will take place every 12 hours, and only the top 75 stakers will qualify for the 2.5x bonus.

But that’s not all.
We will host daily X Spaces lasting between 3 to 6 hours. Everyone is welcome to join, ask questions, and engage, whether in the Spaces or in the X community.

We will also aim to trend daily on Dexscreener, and I will do everything in my power to take $TRUST to the multi-millions.

All we have to do is TRUST the process and BELIEF in each other.
name: TRUST

description: Тотя. ❤️, sometimes spelled Totr or Totya, is a Russian meme featuring a pair of naked SpongeBobs alongside text that translates to "Totya." The meme began gaining traction in early September 2025 alongside a remix of the song "Stay" by Kid LAROI and Justin Bieber. Тотя memes are often placed in opposition to memes about ПЫ, which features an image of a Minion and potato hybrid. The seemingly nonsensical trend mirrors a series of similar brainrot style memes that gained traction in 2025, including Italian Brainrot Animals and German Brainrot.
name: Totr

description: Collegium is not positioning RWAs as wrapped representations, but as first class programmable assets secured and executed on Base.   The architecture is built around deterministic execution, state isolation, and protocol native compliance, removing reliance on off-chain middleware and fragmented verification layers.At the program layer, Collegium leverages Anchor to enforce strict account validation, deterministic instruction flows, and upgrade-safe program design.
